mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 (including FLT3‑ITD) and PKC signaling to reduce AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Also described as approved for FLT3+ AML; suggested as a positive control/benchmark.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 to suppress AML growth, especially FLT3‑ITD contexts
additional_notes: Approved for HCC/RCC; noted precedent in FLT3‑ITD AML/relapse settings.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family kinases and BCR‑ABL, suppressing proliferative signaling networks
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; noted activity in AML subsets.

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition; reported activity against some FLT3 variants in certain contexts
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; text notes high potency (top 0.3 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits receptor tyrosine kinases to reduce AML cell growth
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/GIST; described as broad RTK inhibition with reported in vitro anti-AML effects.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases, leading to growth inhibition in AML cells in some studies
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/STS; range given as 10 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Selectively inhibits BCL‑2 to induce apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; noted high potency and to keep DMSO low; MOLM‑13 variably sensitive.

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL‑2 and BCL‑XL to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Clinical-stage; suggested as comparator to venetoclax (addresses BCL‑XL contribution).

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter transcription/epigenetic state and suppress AML proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; noted potential synergy with FLT3 inhibitors; described as potent (top 0.3 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to promote growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Also listed as SAHA (alternative name); approved for CTCL.

mechanism: Weak HDAC inhibition leading to differentiation/epigenetic modulation
additional_notes: Approved for epilepsy; noted weaker potency; requires aqueous handling and osmolarity controls.

mechanism: Incorporates into nucleic acids and inhibits DNMTs, leading to DNA hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Approved for AML/MDS; described as standard-of-care comparator; MOLM‑13 variably responsive.

mechanism: Inhibits DNA methyltransferase (DNMT) via incorporation into DNA, causing hypomethylation
additional_notes: Approved for AML/MDS; noted as a positive control in epigenetic screens.

mechanism: Purine nucleoside analog that disrupts DNA synthesis/repair leading to cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Approved for hairy cell leukemia; noted use in AML regimens (e.g., CLAG).

mechanism: Purine nucleoside analog that inhibits DNA synthesis, causing cytotoxicity to proliferating blasts
additional_notes: Approved for CLL; described as antimetabolite comparator.

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and impairs autophagosome-lysosome fusion, inhibiting autophagy
additional_notes: Approved for malaria/autoimmune disease; noted to watch for nonspecific toxicity at high end.

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and disrupts autophagy/lysosomal function
additional_notes: Approved for malaria; noted sometimes more potent than hydroxychloroquine in vitro.

mechanism: Polypharmacology reported to inhibit Wnt/STAT3/mTOR pathway signaling
additional_notes: Approved for tapeworm; solubility can be limiting; verify; noted high protein binding in general notes.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubules to inhibit cell division
additional_notes: Approved for helminths; solubility/precipitation common—confirm by visual inspection.

mechanism: Reported ALDH/proteasome/NF‑κB-related effects; copper-dependent cytotoxicity described in AML
additional_notes: Alcohol aversion drug; optional condition: ± CuCl₂ 0.5–2 µM to test copper dependence.
